title: Washington: no general deletion right, but strong health-data rights
summary: Washington has not enacted a comprehensive consumer-privacy law with a general right to delete. But its My Health My Data Act gives a strong right to access and to delete consumer health data held by many businesses — broadly defined, beyond traditional medical records. For general removal, use the data broker's own opt-out (below).
disclaimer: General information, not legal advice, and not yet independently reviewed. The My Health My Data Act applies to health data specifically; verify scope and process before relying on it.
